title: Gyalyum Chenmo Memorial Gold Cup (GCMGC)
content: The Gyalyum Chenmo Memorial Gold Cup (GCMGC) is the most prestigious football tournament in the Tibetan diaspora community. Named in honor of the Great Mother (Gyalyum Chenmo), the tournament has been a cornerstone of Tibetan sports culture for over three decades, bringing together teams from settlements across India, Nepal, and beyond.

The GCMGC is organized annually by the Tibetan National Sports Association (TNSA) and typically features 16 teams divided into four groups, competing through group stages, quarter-finals, semi-finals, and a grand final over approximately 10 days of intense competition.

The historic 30th edition was held at Upper TCV grounds, where DYSA Mundgod was crowned champions after defeating TDL Bylakuppe in a thrilling final. The tournament drew large crowds, with all matches broadcast live on the TNSA YouTube channel.

Beyond the competition itself, the GCMGC serves as a unifying event for the Tibetan community. It is the primary stage for scouting talent for the Tibetan National Football Team, with standout performers shortlisted for international competitions such as the CONIFA Asia Cup.

The tournament has also become a platform for cultural initiatives, including the launch of the Tibetan Football Terms Book and new national team jerseys. TNSA continues to introduce eco-friendly practices, such as water refill stations, to promote sustainability at the event.

title: Tongsten Cup
content: The School Tournament, organized under the Tongtsen Cup banner, is an Under-15 boys and girls football tournament that represents a major milestone in TNSA's grassroots football development program. The tournament is aimed at identifying and nurturing young talent from Tibetan schools.

The inaugural Tongtsen Cup was held at Upper TCV football ground, featuring multiple days of group stage matches followed by semi-finals and finals. In the boys' category, STS Dholanji claimed the championship title with TCV Gopalpur finishing as runners-up. The girls' category saw TCV Suja emerge as champions.

All matches are broadcast live on the TNSA Dharamsala YouTube channel, giving young athletes a professional experience and wider visibility. The tournament also features educational workshops, including a Women Empowerment workshop organized for participants.

The Tongtsen Cup is part of TNSA's long-term strategy to build a pipeline of talent for Tibetan football from the grassroots level, providing school-age athletes with a competitive platform to develop their skills and sportsmanship.

title: GCM International
content: The GCM International is a football tournament that brings Tibetan football to the diaspora community outside India, primarily in North America. The tournament extends the spirit of the Gyalyum Chenmo Memorial Gold Cup to Tibetan communities worldwide.

The GCM International is set to return to New York in 2026, generating excitement among Tibetan football fans around the world. The tournament provides a platform for diaspora teams from the United States, Canada, and other countries to compete and celebrate Tibetan sporting culture.

By hosting the tournament internationally, TNSA strengthens the connection between Tibetan communities across the globe through sport, giving diaspora youth an opportunity to engage with their heritage and fellow Tibetans in a competitive yet communal setting.

The GCM International complements TNSA's broader efforts to expand Tibetan football beyond South Asia, including the historic first Tibetan U11 and U13 youth soccer tournament held in the United States in collaboration with Tibet FC Grassroots Soccer.

title: Chikdril Gold Cup
content: The Chikdril Gold Cup is a premier football tournament organized by TNSA featuring both men's and women's categories, providing a competitive platform for athletes from Tibetan settlements across India.

sections.label: Men
content: The Men's Chikdril Gold Cup brings together top men's football teams from Tibetan settlements for an exciting knockout-style competition.

The tournament promotes physical fitness, teamwork, and community bonding among Tibetan athletes. Teams from various settlements compete in a spirited atmosphere that showcases the best of Tibetan men's football.
The Chikdril Gold Cup is closely tied to TNSA's broader development programs, serving as a key scouting platform for identifying talented players for national team selection.
The tournament reflects TNSA's belief that sport is a powerful tool for community building and maintaining an active lifestyle within the Tibetan community.

sections.label: Women
content: The Women's Chikdril Gold Cup, also known as the Tibet Cup for Women, is a premier women's football tournament organized by TNSA to promote and develop women's football within the Tibetan community.

The tournament features women's and boys' categories, providing a competitive platform for female athletes from Tibetan settlements across India. Teams compete in group stages followed by knockout rounds, with matches typically held at Mundgod.

In the 2026 edition, Munsel claimed the championship title, with Longsho finishing as runners-up after a hard-fought final. The 2025 edition (2nd edition) saw Munsel win the women's crown and Red Devils take the boys' title.
The tournament represents TNSA's commitment to gender equality in sport, giving women the opportunity to showcase their talent and compete at a high level.

title: Climate Cup
content: The Climate Cup is a football tournament that combines competitive sport with environmental awareness. The tournament brings together teams to compete while raising awareness about climate change and sustainability.
Organized with an eco-friendly approach, the Climate Cup reflects TNSA's commitment to promoting environmental consciousness through sport within the Tibetan community.

title: CONIFA (World Cup)
content: The CONIFA World Cup is the flagship tournament of the Confederation of Independent Football Associations, bringing together national teams from unrecognized nations, de facto states, and regions around the world.

sections.label: Men
content: The Tibetan Men's National Football Team has represented Tibet at the CONIFA World Cup, competing against teams from around the globe. The tournament provides an invaluable platform for Tibetan players to gain international experience and showcase their talent on the world stage.
Participation in the CONIFA World Cup is a highlight of TNSA's international program, reinforcing the visibility of Tibetan football and the broader Tibetan cause through sport.

sections.label: Women
content: The Tibetan Women's National Football Team has participated in the CONIFA World Cup, marking a significant milestone for women's football in the Tibetan community.
Competing internationally provides invaluable experience for Tibetan women footballers and inspires the next generation of female athletes in the diaspora.

title: CONIFA (Asian Cup)
content: The CONIFA Asian Cup is the continental championship organized by the Confederation of Independent Football Associations for Asian member teams.

sections.label: Men
content: The Tibetan Men's National Football Team competes in the CONIFA Asian Cup, facing teams from across the continent. The tournament serves as a crucial stepping stone for international competition and helps raise the profile of Tibetan football in Asia.

Players selected for the Asian Cup squad are drawn from standout performers at domestic tournaments like the GCMGC and the Tibetan Football League, ensuring the best talent represents Tibet on the international stage.

sections.label: Women
content: The Tibetan Women's National Football Team participates in the CONIFA Asian Cup, competing against women's teams from across Asia.
The tournament provides critical international match experience for Tibetan women footballers and supports TNSA's mission of developing women's football at all levels.

title: Governor's Gold Cup
content: The Governor's Gold Cup is a prestigious football tournament held in Sikkim, India. The Tibetan National Football Team has participated in this tournament, competing against local and regional teams.

Participation in the Governor's Gold Cup provides the Tibetan team with competitive match experience against Indian football clubs and state-level teams, helping to raise the standard of Tibetan football.

title: Unity Cup
content: The Unity Cup is a women's football tournament that brings together diverse teams in a celebration of sport and solidarity. The Tibetan Women's National Football Team has participated in this tournament, gaining valuable international experience.
The tournament embodies the spirit of unity through sport, providing Tibetan women footballers with a platform to compete, connect, and represent their community on the international stage.
